theme: AI-Powered Environment Variable Suggestions + Framework Detection
context: In v1.5.0 the LLM detects secrets. In v1.7.0 it starts suggesting what variables you need — before you even know you need them. This is the biggest feature leap toward v2.0.

tasks.title: Framework Detection Engine
tasks.description: Auto-detect the project framework by reading package.json dependencies + scanning file structure. Return a confidence score and the detected framework name. Used as context for AI suggestions.
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Correct framework detected for 10+ popular stacks with 90%+ accuracy

tasks.title: Code Analysis Engine
tasks.description: Scan all workspace files for process.env.VARIABLE_NAME usage (JS/TS), os.environ.get('VAR') (Python), env('VAR') (PHP/Laravel), ENV['VAR'] (Ruby). Compare found variables against the current .env file to identify which are missing, which are unused, and which have no value.

outputs.used_in_code: list of all env var names referenced in code
outputs.missing: used in code but not in .env
outputs.unused: in .env but not used in code

tasks.acceptance_criteria: Correctly identifies missing and unused variables across JS/TS/Python projects

tasks.title: Common Patterns Database
tasks.description: Build a static database of known environment variable patterns for 30+ popular services. Each entry includes: canonical variable name, description, example value format, and which framework typically uses it.
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Database covers 30+ services with correct variable names and example values

tasks.title: LLM Suggestion Endpoint — POST /suggest
tasks.description: New Railway endpoint that receives detected framework, list of env vars used in code, list of vars already in .env, and list of missing vars. Returns AI-generated suggestions: variable name, description, example value, reason why it is needed, and confidence score.

suggestions.name: STRIPE_SECRET_KEY
suggestions.description: Stripe secret key for server-side payment processing

tasks.acceptance_criteria: Endpoint responds in under 2 seconds. Suggestions match framework context.

tasks.title: AI Suggestions Panel Section
tasks.description: Add a new collapsible 'AI Suggestions' card to the environment webview panel. Shows a list of suggested missing variables with: variable name, description, example value, reason detected, confidence badge, and Add to .env button.
ui_components: Variable row: name + description + example + confidence badge
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Suggestions panel renders correctly, shows loading state, and updates after adding a variable

tasks.title: One-Click Variable Addition from Suggestions
tasks.description: When user clicks Add on a suggested variable: append VARIABLE_NAME=example_value to the active .env file, trigger a file re-scan, remove the variable from the suggestions list, and show a toast notification. Support undo via VS Code edit history.
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Variable added to .env within 500ms. Undo works. Suggestions list updates immediately.

tasks.title: Auto-Fix Code Actions via VS Code Quick Fix
tasks.description: When a secret is detected in code (e.g. hardcoded Stripe key), register a VS Code diagnostic and Quick Fix code action: 'Replace with process.env.STRIPE_SECRET_KEY'. Clicking it replaces the hardcoded value in the source file AND adds the variable to .env with a placeholder value.
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Yellow lightbulb appears on secret detection. Quick Fix correctly replaces value in source. Variable added to .env.

priority_guide.P0: Must have — foundation for all AI features
priority_guide.P2: Nice to have — include if time permits

success_criteria: Framework detected correctly for 10+ popular stacks
Missing variables identified correctly from code scan
LLM /suggest endpoint responds in under 2 seconds
Suggestions accepted by user in 70%+ of cases
One-click fix adds variable to .env and removes from suggestions
Quick Fix lightbulb appears on all detected secrets
